reportingFrame.applicability: Disease-specific GBD burden estimates across locations and time.
reportingFrame.reviewStatus: The query design has a matching source export and provenance-backed derived summary. It is manuscript-candidate-ready but not submission-ready until citation, reuse, journal wording, and human review gates pass.

metricPolicy.rateCountPercentMixingBoundary: All-age counts and age-standardized rates must be written as separate claims. Do not combine number and rate changes in one sentence unless the transformation is explicitly defined.

ageStandardization.evidenceRequirement: Age-standardized rows must be present for rate trend claims. All ages rows may support burden counts only.
ageStandardization.allAgesInterpretationRule: All ages is a count category for this scaffold, not an age-standardized estimate.
ageStandardization.boundary: Do not compare China and global all-age rates unless both rows are confirmed to have the same age category and metric; prefer age-standardized rates for location comparisons.

uncertaintyIntervalPolicy.narrativePolicy: Every numeric CVD claim must carry uncertainty intervals from the exact GBD export rows. Avoid confidence-interval, p-value, causal, mechanism, or policy-effect wording.
uncertaintyIntervalPolicy.reviewReadyRule: Each claim must identify measure, metric, cause, location, age, sex, year, val, lower, and upper.
uncertaintyIntervalPolicy.submissionBoundary: Submission-ready claims require journal-specific UI wording and source-row traceability.

citationPolicy.source: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME), Global Health Data Exchange (GHDx), GBD Results Tool
citationPolicy.manuscriptCitationDraft: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME). GBD Results Tool. Global Health Data Exchange. GBD 2023, version_id 8352. Accessed 2026-06-11. https://vizhub.healthdata.org/gbd-results/
citationPolicy.exportCitationDraft: Global Burden of Disease Collaborative Network. Global Burden of Disease Study 2023 (GBD 2023) Results. Seattle, United States: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME), 2024. Available from https://vizhub.healthdata.org/gbd-results/.
citationPolicy.recommendedManuscriptCitation: Global Burden of Disease Collaborative Network. Global Burden of Disease Study 2023 (GBD 2023) Results. Seattle, United States: Institute for Health Metrics and Evaluation (IHME), 2024. Available from https://vizhub.healthdata.org/gbd-results/. Accessed 2026-06-11.
citationPolicy.finalSubmissionRequirement: Confirm journal/IHME wording and reuse terms before submission-ready status.

reuseBoundary.rule: Do not commit large GBD exports or package derivative datasets until IHME/GHDx terms are confirmed for the exact export.
reuseBoundary.repositoryPolicy: This scaffold may be committed because it contains query metadata only. Source exports should remain small, provenance-tracked, and terms-compliant.
reuseBoundary.repositoryDecision: This instance keeps a small manually exported CSV plus derived aggregate summary as a review fixture, but final public release requires human confirmation of IHME/GHDx reuse terms.
reuseBoundary.releasePackagingDecision: Do not package the raw source export into release artifacts until reuse is confirmed; derived aggregate outputs may be packaged only with citation and reuse review notes.
reuseBoundary.submissionBoundary: Submission-ready status requires human confirmation that the exact export, derived tables, and manuscript wording comply with IHME/GHDx and journal requirements.

blockingRules: Missing CVD-specific source export blocks numeric claims.
Missing age-standardized rows blocks age-standardized trend or location-comparison claims.
Missing lower/upper source fields blocks uncertainty-aware GBD claims.
Metric type ambiguity blocks rate/count comparisons.
Missing citation or reuse confirmation blocks submission packaging.
